2013-10-01.title: Greece: A Vow to Erase a ‘Shame’
2013-10-01.abstract: Prime Minister Antonis Samaras suggested on Monday that he wanted to eradicate Golden Dawn, the neo-fascist xenophobic political party that is facing a government crackdown.

2013-10-01.title: Israel: Egyptian Rescuer Is Honored
2013-10-01.abstract: Mohamed Helmy, an Egyptian doctor who hid a Jewish woman in a cabin in Berlin during World War II, became the first Arab honored by Israel’s Holocaust memorial.

2013-10-01.title: Indonesia: Australian Leader Aims to Halt Flow of Asylum Seekers
2013-10-01.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Abbott of Australia pledged Monday to work with Indonesia to stop the flow of people traveling by boat to Australia to seek asylum.

2013-10-01.title: Iran Staggers as Sanctions Hit Economy
2013-10-01.abstract: A currency shortage, created by Western sanctions, appears worse than previously thought, increasing pressure on leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.

2013-10-01.title: A Football Match Where Hooligans Take a Back Seat to Vikings
2013-10-01.abstract: At Britain’s national stadium, Wembley, a sold-out crowd of 83,500 people watched a close game, as the Minnesota Vikings won a first victory over the Pittsburgh Steelers, 34-27.

2013-10-02.title: Jellyfish Invasion Paralyzes Swedish Reactor
2013-10-02.abstract: Reflecting a global problem, a cluster of moon jellyfish clogged the cooling water intake pipes of the world’s largest boiling-water nuclear reactor, in southeastern Sweden, forcing its shutdown.

2013-10-02.title: Losing Hope in Fukushima
2013-10-02.abstract: More than two years after the nuclear meltdown in Fukushima Prefecture, thousands of refugees, desperate to return home, are losing confidence in the Japanese government’s cleanup efforts.

2013-10-02.title: Near Fukushima, a Human Crisis Quietly Unfolds
2013-10-02.abstract: The 83,000 refugees evacuated from the worst-hit areas around the nuclear power plant are still unable to go home, two and a half years after the disaster.

2013-10-02.title: Pope Assails Bureaucracy of Church as Insular
2013-10-02.abstract: In his second revealing interview in two weeks, Pope Francis called the Roman Catholic Church overly clerical and insular, interested in temporal power and often led by “narcissists.”

2013-10-02.title: Japan’s Nuclear Refugees, Still Stuck in Limbo
2013-10-02.abstract: Tens of thousands of evacuees from the area around the Fukushima nuclear plant are in emotional and legal limbo as the cleanup limps along.

2013-10-03.title: Weapons Inspection Team Begins Work in Syria
2013-10-03.abstract: A team of weapons inspectors has begun the groundwork for destroying Syria’s chemical arms, but it is unclear how it will navigate the complex task amid a civil war.

2013-10-03.title: France: Court Throws Out Cases Claiming Racial Profiling by Police
2013-10-03.abstract: A court in Paris threw out lawsuits that claimed illegal racial profiling by the police engaged in public identity checks and pat-downs.

2013-10-03.title: Iran’s President Responds to Netanyahu
2013-10-03.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani of Iran dismissed the verbal assault on his credibility by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel the day before at the United Nations General Assembly.

2013-10-03.title: On Fashion Runway, South Sudan Takes Steps Toward a National Identity
2013-10-03.abstract: With her fashion shows, Akuja de Garang is trying to change perceptions of South Sudan, including those of its own people, even as interethnic tension and violence persist.

2013-10-03.title: U.S. and Japan Agree to Broaden Military Alliance
2013-10-03.abstract: The agreement, which includes a new missile defense system in Japan and coordination on cyberthreats, signals the United States’ increased focus on Asia.

2013-10-04.title: Russia: A Legal Victory for Kasparov
2013-10-04.abstract: Garry Kasparov, the chess champion and opposition politician, on Thursday won a ruling against the Russian government for an illegal arrest at a Moscow rally in 2007.

2013-10-04.title: Russia: More Charges for Greenpeace
2013-10-04.abstract: Prosecutors have now pressed piracy charges against the entire crew of a Greenpeace International ship, including the captain; the ship’s cook and doctor; and two freelance journalists.

2013-10-04.title: Britain: Online Surveillance Challenged
2013-10-04.abstract: Three British organizations said they had filed a legal challenge to the online surveillance programs of the British spy agency the Government Communications Headquarters.

2013-10-04.title: U.S. and Israel Share a Goal in Iran Talks, but Not a Strategy
2013-10-04.abstract: The stated goal is to stop Iran from developing a nuclear weapon, but the mantra of the Israeli prime minister is “distrust, dismantle and verify,” while President Obama focuses on verifying the peaceful nature of the program.

2013-10-04.title: Amid Bloodshed in Pakistan, a Stock Exchange Soars
2013-10-04.abstract: Pakistan’s commercial capital, Karachi, has been plagued by political, militant and criminal violence, but some investors say that masks the good bargains to be found.

2013-10-05.title: The Netherlands: Greenpeace Activists Gain an Ally
2013-10-05.abstract: The Netherlands intends to press a case against Russia in an international tribunal for seizing a Greenpeace International ship which was sailing under a Dutch flag.

2013-10-05.title: 6 Die in Egypt as Islamist Opposition Tries to Re-energize Movement
2013-10-05.abstract: A day of clashes between protest groups in Egypt was seen as a warm-up for a day of demonstrations set for Sunday.

2013-10-05.title: Hagel’s Week in Asia, Complete With Talk of Drones and Fiscal Standoff
2013-10-05.abstract: Defense Secretary Chuck Hagel weighed in on the government shutdown, drones and Iran as he traveled to South Korea and Japan.

2013-10-05.title: Video of Nairobi Gunmen
2013-10-05.abstract: Kenyan officials have used footage from the Westgate mall to identify four suspects.

2013-10-05.title: Migrants Cast Their Lot on a Troubled Sea
2013-10-05.abstract: A rising number of migrants from Africa and the Middle East are daring the waters of the Mediterranean to reach Europe.

2013-10-06.title: Plan for Ridding Syria of Chemical Arms Includes Brute Force and Chemistry
2013-10-06.abstract: A plan aims to prevent backsliding by Syria’s government and limit the time that international experts need to work in the country.

2013-10-06.title: Grand Jury Indictment of Abu Anas al-Libi
2013-10-06.abstract: American forces captured a Libyan militant, known by his nom de guerre, Abu Anas al-Libi, who had been indicted in 2000 for his role in the 1998 bombings of the United States embassies in Kenya and Tanzania.

2013-10-06.title: Irish Say No to No Senate in Blow to Prime Minister
2013-10-06.abstract: A measure to abolish the upper house of Parliament, which Prime Minister Enda Kenny had championed, was widely expected to pass.

2013-10-06.title: Gunfire Wounds Israeli Girl, 9, Playing in Yard in West Bank
2013-10-06.abstract: Israeli authorities said they suspected that the attack was carried out by a Palestinian who had broken into the settlement, Psagot, southeast of the Palestinian city of Ramallah.

2013-10-06.title: As Germans Push Austerity, Greeks Press Nazi-Era Claims
2013-10-06.abstract: Resentment over Germany’s power to dictate budget austerity to Greece has some Greeks talking about settling debts from World War II.

2013-10-07.title: Pakistan Army Chief Says He Will Retire Next Month
2013-10-07.abstract: Despite media speculation to the contrary, Gen. Ashfaq Parvez Kayani confirmed he will step down on Nov. 29, creating an opening for a new army chief.

2013-10-07.title: 4 Held in Iran Nuclear Site Plot
2013-10-07.abstract: Four people, most likely employees of Iran’s nuclear program, were suspected of planning to sabotage one of its nuclear sites, an official told reporters.

2013-10-07.title: Rival Factions in Strike Underscore the Fissures in Post-Chávez Venezuela
2013-10-07.abstract: The government claims a strike at a steel mill was prompted by American meddling, but strikers say they are owed unpaid bonuses and other benefits.

2013-10-07.title: Target in U.S. Raid on Somalia Is Called Top Shabab Planner of Attacks Abroad
2013-10-07.abstract: The man, a Kenyan of Somali origin known as Ikrimah, was not captured, and there is as yet no evidence that he was killed in the firefight on the Somali coast early Saturday.

2013-10-07.title: News of Argentine President’s Latest Ailment Fuels Criticism and Speculation
2013-10-07.abstract: The revelation on Saturday that President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner suffered a head injury seven weeks ago has fueled questions about the government’s actions.

2013-10-08.title: Meeting With Israelis, Palestinian Leader Strikes a Conciliatory Tone on Peace Talks
2013-10-08.abstract: President Mahmoud Abbas of the Palestinian Authority expressed optimism that a peace agreement could be reached in the nine months allotted to the current round of talks.

2013-10-08.title: Britain Replaces Minister Responsible for Scotland
2013-10-08.abstract: Less than a year before Scots vote on independence, the move was apparently aimed to give a harder edge to the campaign against Britain’s breakup.

2013-10-08.title: Argentina: Surgery for President
2013-10-08.abstract: President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner will undergo surgery  to drain an accumulation of blood near her brain resulting from a recent head injury, her doctors said.

2013-10-08.title: China: Big Energy Project Planned
2013-10-08.abstract: China is planning to build its largest coal gasification energy project in the western region of Xinjiang, according to a report by Xinhua, the state news agency.

2013-10-08.title: Iraq: Outburst of Bombings Sweeps Through Baghdad Neighborhoods
2013-10-08.abstract: Twelve explosions hit Shiite neighborhoods in Baghdad within 40 minutes on Monday, killing at least 37 people and wounding more than 100.

2013-10-09.title: China: Police Fire on Tibetan Protesters, Report Says
2013-10-09.abstract: A Tibet advocacy group and Radio Free Asia said Chinese security forces had fired on a protest in an eastern town in the Tibet Autonomous Region, injuring at least 60 people.

2013-10-09.title: China: Decision in Bo Xilai Appeal Expected Soon
2013-10-09.abstract: Bo Xilai, a former senior Communist Party official who was sentenced to life in prison last month, expects a court in eastern China to make a decision soon on his appeal, a family associate said.

2013-10-09.title: Britain and Iran Move to Repair Diplomatic Relations
2013-10-09.abstract: Britain and Iran announced they would each appoint a chargé d’affaires, a step below ambassador, to work toward resuming full diplomatic ties, including reopening embassies.

2013-10-09.title: Rights Advocates Suing U.N. Over the Spread of Cholera in Haiti
2013-10-09.abstract: Advocates for Haitian victims of a cholera epidemic that afflicted the country three years ago are suing the United Nations, saying its peacekeepers introduced the disease through sewage contamination.

2013-10-09.title: Obama Expected to Reduce Military Aid to Egypt
2013-10-09.abstract: The anticipated move will hold up weapons delivery, but not counterterrorism aid, in the wake of a deadly military crackdown on the Muslim Brotherhood.

2013-10-10.title: Cutbacks in U.N. Food Assistance Set Off Outcry in Gaza
2013-10-10.abstract: The United Nations Relief and Works Agency pointed to a $55 million budget shortfall and said the cuts were based on a re-evaluation of Gaza’s needs.

2013-10-10.title: A Russian Region Neither at War Nor at Peace, but Facing a Crackdown
2013-10-10.abstract: Russian authorities, determined that nothing will mar the Olympics in Sochi, have been accused of human rights violations.

2013-10-10.title: As Drilling Practice Takes Off in U.S., Europe Proves Hesitant
2013-10-10.abstract: The temptation to follow the United States in extracting shale gas from rock presents Europeans with contentious trade-offs.

2013-10-10.title: Extremist Group Gains Foothold Among Kenyans
2013-10-10.abstract: Kenya’s slums have long provided a fertile recruiting ground for Muslim extremists, but analysts say that the Shabab have been finding recruits from across the country, not just in traditionally Muslim areas.

2013-10-10.title: Fairness an Issue, Even After Azerbaijan Votes
2013-10-10.abstract: Jamil Hasanli, who appeared headed to defeat in Azerbaijan’s presidential election, said a compressed campaign calendar was one obstacle in his attempt to deny Ilham Aliyev a third term.

2013-10-11.title: C.I.A. Warning on Snowden in ’09 Said to Slip Through the Cracks
2013-10-11.abstract: When Edward J. Snowden worked as a C.I.A. technician in 2009, he was suspected of trying to break into classified files, but a report was never forwarded when he left to become an N.S.A. contractor, officials said.

2013-10-11.title: U.N. Questions Criticism of Its Peacekeepers
2013-10-11.abstract: The United Nations said it would consider a report asserting that insufficient attention is paid to corruption among its troops, but it challenged the methodology used by the authors.

2013-10-11.title: Fifth Suspect Is Indicted in 2005 Killing of Ex-Lebanese Premier
2013-10-11.abstract: An international tribunal handed down another indictment against a member of Hezbollah in the bombing that killed Rafik Hariri, the former Lebanese prime minister.

2013-10-11.title: Mother to Visit American Held in North Korea
2013-10-11.abstract: The mother of Kenneth Bae, who was sentenced to 15 years of hard labor, has been granted permission to visit him and arrived in Pyongyang on Thursday.

2013-10-11.title: Syrian Civilians Bore Brunt of Rebels’ Fury, Report Says
2013-10-11.abstract: Human Rights Watch said it had documented for the first time that rebel groups in Syria “systematically” targeted civilians in a massacre in August that left 190 dead.

2013-10-12.title: Anger Lingers in Sudan After a Crackdown
2013-10-12.abstract: The government used what it called an “iron fist” to suppress protests over the lifting of gasoline subsidies, killing many and jailing hundreds. Now detainees are sharing accounts of the abuse they faced.

2013-10-12.title: India Braces for Cyclone Expected on East Coast
2013-10-12.abstract: A monstrous cyclone that may be among the most powerful storms ever recorded in the Bay of Bengal was expected to hit the eastern coast of India by Saturday night.

2013-10-12.title: Britain: Rules for Press Proposed
2013-10-12.abstract: Britain’s three main political parties on Friday agreed on a plan to regulate the country’s newspapers, but the proposal faced heavy criticism from the press.

2013-10-12.title: Venezuela Intercepts Ship With 5 Americans Aboard
2013-10-12.abstract: The Americans, who were on a research ship working under an oil exploration contract with the Guyanese government, appear to have been ensnared in a territorial dispute.

2013-10-12.title: C.I.A. Disputes Early Suspicions on Snowden
2013-10-12.abstract: The C.I.A. said that it did not suspect Edward J. Snowden of gaining access to computer files without authorization when he was working as a technician for the agency in 2009.

2013-10-13.title: Behind Flurry of Killing, Potency of Hate
2013-10-13.abstract: A former assassin with the Irish Republican Army has particular insight into a recent flurry of horrific bloodletting in the world.

2013-10-13.title: Family Man One Day, Rebel Fighter the Next
2013-10-13.abstract: Thousands of men who once held ordinary jobs in Syria have turned into part-time fighters, leaving their refugee families to return to the war.

2013-10-13.title: Quick Evacuation Efforts Help India Minimize Deaths From Cyclone
2013-10-13.abstract: Reports after a powerful storm struck the country’s eastern coast showed the success of an operation that moved more than 800,000 people to safety.

2013-10-13.title: Asia’s Lands of Charm and Cruelty
2013-10-13.abstract: Close calls, lessons learned: Tales from two decades of traveling and listening.

2013-10-13.title: Tunnel Found From Gaza Into Israel, Military Says
2013-10-13.abstract: Responding to the discovery, the Israeli military said it had suspended the flow of building materials into Gaza because the tunnel could have been used for an attack against soldiers or civilians.

2013-10-14.title: Setting Pace, Ireland Predicts December Exit From Bailout
2013-10-14.abstract: A successful completion of an austerity program would be a significant psychological threshold for the 17-nation euro zone.

2013-10-14.abstract: Hundreds of civilians were allowed to leave Moadhamiyeh, outside Damascus, but aid workers said they were still unable to enter the besieged town, where six people have reportedly died of malnutrition.

2013-10-14.abstract: A growing urbanized middle class wants politicians facing charges driven from the system, but tribal voters see some, like Kameshwar Baitha, as heroes.

2013-10-14.abstract: Driving in Syria reveals the sometimes surreal experience of Syrians’ trying to move themselves and their goods around a country that has become a patchwork of rebellion and control.

2013-10-14.title: To Ousted Boss, Arms Watchdog Was Seen as an Obstacle in Iraq
2013-10-14.abstract: A former head of the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons said he was ousted more than a decade ago because of pressure from the Bush administration over inspections in Iraq.

2013-10-15.abstract: Government efforts to silence a Peking University professor who has criticized Communist rule come as China’s colleges are ambitiously seeking respect abroad as great centers of learning.

2013-10-15.title: No Quarter for Former SS Captain, Reviled Even in Death
2013-10-15.abstract: Officials in Argentina, Germany and Italy have blocked the burial of Erich Priebke, who was sentenced for helping to organize the execution of 335 civilians in Rome in 1944.

2013-10-15.title: Chinese Bodyguards, When Police Won’t Do
2013-10-15.abstract: Threats to wealthy Chinese at home and abroad have spurred the development of a personal security industry.

2013-10-15.title: A Proposed Movie Deal for a Piracy Suspect Has a Surprise Ending: His Arrest
2013-10-15.abstract: A suspect behind a 2009 attack on a Belgian ship was lured to Brussels with an offer to make a film about his piracy past, the authorities said.

2013-10-15.title: Mark B. Read, Criminal Turned Author, Dies at 58
2013-10-15.abstract: Mr. Read, known as Chopper, based top-selling books and stand-up performance on his violent and varied life of crime.

2013-10-16.title: Group Says Haitian Garment Workers Are Shortchanged on Pay
2013-10-16.abstract: The Worker Rights Consortium reported the “theft” of part of the income legally due to the workers.

2013-10-16.title: Death in Hong Kong Fuels Feelings of Discrimination
2013-10-16.abstract: The death of a mainland Chinese woman in Hong Kong has revived tensions between mainland Chinese and Hong Kong locals.

2013-10-16.title: A Muzzled Chinese Artwork, Absent but Speaking Volumes
2013-10-16.abstract: A retrospective in Beijing for the Chinese sculptor Wang Keping reflects a chill in expression.

2013-10-16.title: Funeral for Ex-Nazi in Italy Is Halted as Protesters Clash
2013-10-16.abstract: But even as Erich Priebke’s death under house arrest reopened wounds in Italy, there were calls, too, for toning down the vitriol.

2013-10-16.title: Viewing U.S. in Fear and Dismay
2013-10-16.abstract: The world has reacted mostly with disbelief that a superpower could fall into such dysfunction.

2013-10-17.title: British Police on Defensive Over Downing Street Clash
2013-10-17.abstract: A year after a minister was forced to quit the cabinet over an altercation with the police, new evidence has cast doubt on the police’s account.

2013-10-17.title: Assault of Dutch Diplomat in Moscow Frays Ties Between Russia and the Netherlands
2013-10-17.abstract: The Russian Foreign Ministry tried to head off any political fallout from the attack in the diplomat’s apartment by promising to detain the two assailants.

2013-10-17.title: Hugh White on ‘The China Choice’
2013-10-17.abstract: In a new book, Hugh White, a former Australian government defense official and now a professor of strategic studies at Australian National University, argues that by accommodating China, the United States would reduce what he sees as the escalating chance of war.

2013-10-17.abstract: The abortive evacuation was arranged in a small-scale local deal that some aid workers and others had hoped could become a blueprint for building trust.

2013-10-17.abstract: Exhuming the remains of the notable dead has become increasingly common in Latin American countries. Brazil, the latest, plans to exhume a former civilian president to investigate assassination claims.

2013-10-18.title: Doubts Fall on Afghan Police as Kidnappers Exact Dire Price
2013-10-18.abstract: Even in a war-weary place like Helmand Province, kidnappings for ransom have proved a bigger threat to many people than attacks by insurgents.

2013-10-18.title: Security Council Elects 5 New Members
2013-10-18.abstract: Chad, Chile, Lithuania, Nigeria and Saudi Arabia were elected Thursday to seats on the 15-member United Nations Security Council for two-year terms starting in January.

2013-10-18.title: Israel: Palestinian Is Killed at Army Base
2013-10-18.abstract: Israeli soldiers on Thursday fatally shot a Palestinian man who drove a construction vehicle into an army base near Ramallah in the West Bank, the Israeli military said.

2013-10-18.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey has long backed the Syrian rebels. But Turkey shelled rebel positions this week, signaling concern about the dominance of radical Islamist groups.

2013-10-18.title: Questions Surround Mt. Everest Tragedy
2013-10-18.abstract: The death of an Australian and three Tibetans in an avalanche on Mount Everest is tinged with questions about access to the mountain.

2013-10-19.title: Mexico: Soda-Tax Bill Moves Forward
2013-10-19.abstract: The lower house of Mexico’s Congress has approved a tax on soda and other sugary drinks as part of the government’s fight against the twin epidemics of obesity and diabetes.

2013-10-19.title: Wave of High-Profile Crimes Has Put Malaysians on the Defensive
2013-10-19.abstract: Once one of Asia’s safest cities, Kuala Lumpur now finds that most residents have a story about a purse snatching, a burglary or worse.

2013-10-19.abstract: Kuala Lumpur, the largest city in Malaysia, was once considered one of Asia’s safest cities. Now it’s fighting a rash of burglaries and other crimes.

2013-10-19.title: A Sri Lankan Journalist Eagerly Toes the Line
2013-10-19.abstract: Rajpal Abeynayake stands atop his nation’s journalistic firmament with an uncritical boosterism of the government. He calls himself the Rush Limbaugh of Sri Lanka.

2013-10-19.title: As Iran Shifts, Hard-Liners See Threat to Battle Cry
2013-10-19.abstract: The revolution’s leading slogan, “Death to America,” is becoming a political battleground as a new president steers the country away from a confrontational posture toward the West.

2013-10-20.title: Bomber Tied to Al Qaeda Kills Dozens in Syrian City
2013-10-20.abstract: A suicide bomber driving a truck packed with explosives killed more than 30 people and wounded dozens on a busy road on the outskirts of Hama.

2013-10-20.title: Jamalul Kiram III, Self-Proclaimed Sultan, Dies at 75
2013-10-20.abstract: Mr. Kiram led a quixotic military effort to regain part of the island of Borneo for his family this year, proclaiming: “I’m the poorest sultan in the world.”

2013-10-20.title: Caribbean Nations to Seek Reparations, Putting Price on Damage of Slavery
2013-10-20.abstract: Fourteen countries plan to compile an inventory of the harm caused by the slave trade and then demand an apology and reparations from the former colonial powers Britain, France and the Netherlands.

2013-10-20.title: China’s Mountains of Construction Rubble
2013-10-20.abstract: While the problem is often concealed, with hundreds of millions of tons of waste being quietly dumped on the outskirts of China’s major cities each year, the rate of growth has made it increasingly inescapable.

2013-10-20.title: Vigilantes Defeat Boko Haram in Its Nigerian Base
2013-10-20.abstract: Fed up with the attacks by homegrown Islamist extremists, young informer-vigilantes have driven the group from Maiduguri, the city from where the group sprung.

2013-10-21.title: China’s Arms Industry Makes Global Inroads
2013-10-21.abstract: From drones to frigates to fighter jets, Chinese companies are aggressively pushing sales of high-tech hardware, mostly in the developing world.

2013-10-21.title: Q. and A.: Jia Zhangke on His New Film ‘A Touch of Sin,’ Part 2
2013-10-21.abstract: The director discusses censorship, industry financing and the need for films to reflect everyday life in China.

2013-10-21.title: Gunmen Fire at Church in Egypt; At Least 3 Die
2013-10-21.abstract: The attack appeared to be one of the deadliest in months against Egypt’s Christian minority, security officials said.

2013-10-21.title: Rebel Raid in Oil-Rich South Sudan Leaves Scores Dead
2013-10-21.abstract: The killings took place in the Twic area of Jonglei State, and were the work of rebels led by David Yau Yau, a spokesman said.

2013-10-21.title: 比尔·坎宁安：更加务实
2013-10-21.abstract: 在九月份的纽约时装周中，商业气息与充满想象力的街头穿着形成了对比，街拍风的流行使节日气氛更加浓厚。

2013-10-22.title: Brazil: Consortium Wins Oil Rights
2013-10-22.abstract: The Brazilian authorities on Monday awarded a license to develop oil production in a coveted deep-sea area to a consortium of five companies.

2013-10-22.title: Plan Aims to Fix Sewers, but Its Cost to Residents Leaves a Bad Taste
2013-10-22.abstract: Thames Water plans to use its 14 million customers to finance a new sewer system, but many feel cheated that they will never own the finished tunnel.

2013-10-22.title: Ex-Patients Police Mexico’s Mental Health System
2013-10-22.abstract: An organization consisting of former patients at psychiatric hospitals is demanding better conditions in the Mexican mental health care system, which is considered among the worst in the Americas.

2013-10-22.title: Tradition of Not Voting Keeps Palestinians Politically Powerless in Jerusalem
2013-10-22.abstract: Mayoral candidates in Jerusalem have written off the votes of Palestinians, who mostly shun the polls as part of a longstanding boycott, despite their large numbers.

2013-10-22.title: Angry at Prague, Artist Ensures He’s Understood
2013-10-22.abstract: David Cerny is a sculptor known in the Czech Republic for provocative works that take on the authorities in his country.

2013-10-23.title: Egyptians Abandoning Hope and Now, Reluctantly, Homeland
2013-10-23.abstract: Amid unrelenting turmoil since President Mohamed Morsi’s ouster, many who had postponed an urge to leave say they are joining a new exodus.

2013-10-23.title: Roma Abduction Case Prompts Birth Certificate Review in Greece
2013-10-23.abstract: The police became suspicious last week when they discovered a blond, green-eyed girl known as Maria at a Roma couple’s home in an encampment.

2013-10-23.title: Russia Putting a Strong Arm on Neighbors
2013-10-23.abstract: As Moldova and other former Soviet republics move to align themselves closer to Europe, Russia is pushing to retain its influence.

2013-10-23.title: In a Harvest, Palestinians Cast a Light on Hardship
2013-10-23.abstract: Palestinians and the advocacy groups that support them are using the olive harvest, a beloved annual ritual, to highlight hardships under Israeli occupation.

2013-10-23.title: Greece Suspends State Financing for Far-Right Party
2013-10-23.abstract: Targeting the neo-fascist Golden Dawn, Parliament passed a bill to punish parties whose leaders are charged with serious crimes.

2013-10-24.title: Some Iranians Seek to Renew an Old Chant
2013-10-24.abstract: Several hard-line groups have announced plans for a “Down With U.S.A.” conference next month, with an award for the best photograph, poster, video, song or caricature.

2013-10-24.title: Extravagant Wedding Raises Awkward Questions for a Beijing Official
2013-10-24.abstract: Amid a major anticorruption campaign, a newspaper focuses its gaze on an official’s lavish spending and other goings-on in a village in the capital’s affluent northeastern suburbs.

2013-10-24.title: Left to Languish, Costly Fire Trucks Present a Reminder of Thai Dysfunction
2013-10-24.abstract: As millions of dollars in never-used fire equipment decays in a parking lot, justice against two former officials convicted of malfeasance and bid-rigging remains elusive and questions of bribery remain.

2013-10-24.title: A Homely Relic on Wheels Awakens Nostalgia in Iran
2013-10-24.abstract: The Paykan, once Iran’s national car, is making a comeback, evoking memories of simpler times from the many Iranians who recall riding in it, or pushing it.

2013-10-24.title: 7-Year Low Reported in Piracy Off Somalia
2013-10-24.abstract: The United Nations secretary general attributed the decline to increased international policing and information-sharing, more prosecutions and imprisonment of captured pirates, and greater security steps by commercial shippers.

2013-10-25.title: Ukraine: Boxing Champion Throws His Hat in a New Ring
2013-10-25.abstract: Vitali Klitschko, who is the reigning World Boxing Council heavyweight champion and leads an opposition political party in Ukraine, declared his candidacy for president of Ukraine in an election scheduled for 2015.

2013-10-25.title: French to Strike, but This Time Owners Call It
2013-10-25.abstract: A group representing France’s professional soccer owners plans to cancel matches next month to protest a tax on salaries of more than one million euros.

2013-10-25.title: Allegation of U.S. Spying on Merkel Puts Obama at Crossroads
2013-10-25.abstract: Officials in the Obama administration say that the National Security Agency, in its push to build a global data-gathering network, has rarely weighed the long-term political costs of some of its operations.

2013-10-25.title: Drone Issue Hovers More Than Ever, Even as Strikes Ebb
2013-10-25.abstract: Scrutiny of drones has been driven by pressure from advocacy groups, media leaks and demands in both the United States and Pakistan for greater transparency in the program.

2013-10-25.title: Indignation Over U.S. Spying Spreads in Europe
2013-10-25.abstract: Politicians across the spectrum in Germany and in Europe were indignant at the apparent violation of trust between friends.

2013-10-26.title: Europe Turns Its Eye to Migration Policies, Amid Another Sea Rescue
2013-10-26.abstract: The scale and urgency of the challenge was brought into stark focus by the rescue of more than 700 refugees overnight near Sicily.

2013-10-26.title: Chinese University Defends Outspoken Teacher’s Firing
2013-10-26.abstract: Peking University’s dismissal of an economist, Xia Yeliang, has prompted questions about whether it was politically motivated.

2013-10-26.title: Mexican Writer Mines the Soccer Field for Metaphors
2013-10-26.abstract: Juan Villoro, one of Mexico’s most decorated and esteemed writers, also happens to be a leading soccer analyst, extracting lessons about society and life from the game.

2013-10-26.title: Angry Over Syrian War, Saudis Fault U.S. Policy
2013-10-26.abstract: Saudi Arabia is threatening to break with the United States and pursue a more robust role in supporting the rebellion against the Syrian government. But officials worry about alienating a friend and helping jihadists.

2013-10-26.title: Following Dear Leader, Kim Jong-un Gets Title From University: Dr. Leader
2013-10-26.abstract: Despite his country’s poverty and dysfunction, Kim Jong-un was awarded an honorary doctorate in economics by a Malaysian university.

2013-10-27.title: Rice Offers a More Modest Strategy for Mideast
2013-10-27.abstract: In an interview, Susan E. Rice, President Obama’s national security adviser, said the president’s goal was to avoid having events in the region swallow his foreign-policy agenda.

2013-10-27.title: Momentum Shifts in Congo’s Battle Against Rebels
2013-10-27.abstract: Until last summer, rebels had the upper hand in their battle against the Congolese government. But the government, backed by the United Nations, has brought new leadership and vigor to the fight.

2013-10-27.title: A Reason for Hope in Congo’s Perpetual War
2013-10-27.abstract: Defeats last year jolted both the government of the Democratic Republic of Congo and the United Nations into action, bringing new leadership and vigor to the fight.

2013-10-27.title: Momentum Shifts in Congo’s Battle Against Rebels
2013-10-27.abstract: Until last summer, rebels had the upper hand in their battle against the Congolese government. But the government, backed by the United Nations, has brought new leadership and vigor to the fight.

2013-10-27.title: Chinese Newspaper Apologizes for Articles by Detained Reporter
2013-10-27.abstract: Days after using its front page to demand the release of its detained reporter, the Guangzhou-based New Express said that Chen Yongzhou took money to attack a prominent company.

2013-10-28.title: British Tabloids on Trial, Along With Ex-Editors
2013-10-28.abstract: The trial of former editors in Rupert Murdoch’s media empire is expected to be aggressive and detailed, with the potential for more revelations about the inner workings of British tabloid journalism.

2013-10-28.title: Data Suggests Push to Spy on Merkel Dates to ’02
2013-10-28.abstract: The administration has seemed uncertain about how to handle the reports concerning the German chancellor, who has enjoyed a close rapport with President Obama.

2013-10-28.title: Caught Between Sudans, Region Tries to Pick Side
2013-10-28.abstract: Residents of the Abyei region, on the border of Sudan and South Sudan, voted on which country to be a part of, a move that could have real consequences if tensions are raised.

2013-10-28.title: Video Offered to Back Claim of Cairo Attack
2013-10-28.abstract: Ansar Beit el-Maqdis, a relatively new extremist group, released footage to back its claim that it carried out a suicide attack in Cairo in September.

2013-10-28.title: New Guidelines on Punishing Child Rape Win Praise, and Criticism
2013-10-28.abstract: A Supreme Court opinion has been welcomed by lawyers as improving protections against rape and sexual assault of children, but some say it may have created a damaging gray area by referring to the physical maturity of the victim.

2013-10-29.title: William H. Sullivan, U.S. Ambassador to Volatile Laos and Iran, Is Dead at 90
2013-10-29.abstract: Mr. Sullivan oversaw a secret bombing campaign in Laos and was the last United States ambassador to Iran before the 1979 hostage crisis.

2013-10-29.title: Greece’s Aggressive Pursuit of Tax Evaders Appears to Collect More Anger Than Money
2013-10-29.abstract: New tax collection laws that were intended to cast a broader net have only fueled the public’s ire at the wealthy.

2013-10-29.title: Aqueducts’ Defenders Volunteer to Fill Breach in Upkeep of Park
2013-10-29.abstract: As budget cuts and vandalism have taken a toll on a park of Rome’s ancient aqueducts, volunteers have come forth to help, but they cannot completely fill the gap.

2013-10-29.title: Pentagon Says Shabab Bomb Specialist Is Killed in Missile Strike in Somalia
2013-10-29.abstract: The strike is the latest evidence that the Obama administration has escalated operations against the Shabab after the bloody siege at a shopping mall in Kenya.

2013-10-29.title: Tadeusz Mazowiecki, Ex-Premier of Poland, Dies at 86
2013-10-29.abstract: Mr. Mazowiecki helped form the Solidarity trade union movement and became the first non-Communist to head an Eastern bloc nation since the late 1940s.

2013-10-30.title: Greeks Question Media, and New Voices Pipe Up
2013-10-30.abstract: Amid the Greek financial crisis, small radio stations, magazines and websites take a bigger role in the nation’s civic discussion.

2013-10-30.title: As It Denounces U.S. Spying, Europe Delays Privacy Protection at Home
2013-10-30.abstract: Germany’s acquiescence in a British-led effort to freeze the privacy measures highlighted what appear to be contradictions between the verbal support for privacy among European leaders and their policy decisions.

2013-10-30.title: Spying Known at Top Levels, Officials Say
2013-10-30.abstract: The White House has long been aware in general terms of the National Security Agency’s overseas eavesdropping, the nation’s top spymaster told a House hearing on Tuesday.

2013-10-30.title: As Power Line Grows, So Does Fight Between Ancient and Modern Korea
2013-10-30.abstract: In a symbol of South Korea’s struggle to reconcile its traditions with its hard-charging modern incarnation, a power line has drawn sit-ins from those who say it encroaches on their burial grounds.

2013-10-30.title: Emotional Gulf Renewed as Israel Frees Palestinian Prisoners
2013-10-30.abstract: The Palestinian leadership prepared a hero’s welcome for the 26 long-serving prisoners, who were released as part of a deal to resume peace negotiations with Israel.

2013-10-31.title: A Reminder of a Conflict That Faded Into History
2013-10-31.abstract: Two Mexican history buffs are pushing to preserve the site of a battle in the Mexican-American War where immigrants to the United States defected to fight with Mexico.

2013-10-31.title: Activists Feel Powerful Wrath as Russia Guards Its Arctic Claims
2013-10-31.abstract: The detained crew of a Greenpeace ship appears to have grossly underestimated Russia’s readiness to assert — and expand — its sovereignty in an Arctic region potentially rich with natural resources.

2013-10-31.title: Tap on Merkel Provides Peek at Vast Spy Net
2013-10-31.abstract: The National Security Agency’s monitoring is not limited to politicians like Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany — it also includes party leaders and aides, and it’s all part of efforts to gain an edge over other nations.

2013-10-31.title: Held 3 Years, French Hostages Return Home
2013-10-31.abstract: The four men, workers at a uranium mine in Niger, were seized in September 2010. Al Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb claimed responsibility.

2013-10-31.title: Closing of Live Poultry Markets Is Called Effective in Flu Fight
2013-10-31.abstract: However, experts warn that shutting such markets permanently would be impractical because consumers in many countries demand live birds, and small farmers cannot afford refrigerated slaughterhouses and trucks.
